---
title: "Interactive regressions"
subtitle: "Filter the sample, choose aggregation weights, watch the coefficient move"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
filters:
  - webr
webr:
  packages: ['arrow', 'dplyr', 'fixest', 'ggplot2', 'tidyr', 'broom']
  show-startup-message: false
  show-header-message: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Loading R in your browser.** First-time load takes 5–15 s; subsequent
sessions are instant. All computation runs **client-side** — your filter
choices never leave your machine, and the server holds no state.
:::

## Choose the analytic configuration

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Sample**
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof country_pick = Inputs.select(
  ["(all)","Austria","Germany","Spain","Italy","France",
   "Netherlands","Belgium","Poland","Czech Republic","Greece",
   "Hungary","Sweden","Denmark","Finland","Norway","United Kingdom",
   "Ireland","Portugal","Switzerland","Bulgaria","Romania","Slovakia",
   "Slovenia","Croatia","Estonia","Latvia","Lithuania"],
  {value: "(all)", multiple: true, label: "Country (Ctrl-click for multi)"})

viewof year_range = Inputs.range([1945, 2025], {value: 2010, step: 1, label: "Year ≥"})
```
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Score construction**
```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof model_pick = Inputs.checkbox(
  ["sonnet","gpt41mini","gemini"],
  {value: ["sonnet","gpt41mini","gemini"], label: "Include models"})

viewof within_agg = Inputs.select(
  ["confidence_weighted","plain_mean","median"],
  {value: "confidence_weighted", label: "Within-manifesto"})

viewof between_agg = Inputs.select(
  ["mean","median","leave_worst_out"],
  {value: "mean", label: "Between-model"})

viewof outcome_dim = Inputs.select(
  ["liberalism_overall","lib_political","lib_social","lib_economic","lib_financial_market"],
  {value: "liberalism_overall", label: "Outcome"})
```
:::

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
ojs_define({
  ojs_countries: country_pick,
  ojs_year_min: year_range,
  ojs_models: model_pick,
  ojs_within: within_agg,
  ojs_between: between_agg,
  ojs_outcome: outcome_dim
})
```

## Result

```{webr-r}
#| context: setup
#| label: load-data
# Pre-load aggregated scores and metadata into the WebR session.
# Files are served as static resources from the same site.
library(arrow); library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(fixest); library(ggplot2)

manifestos <- arrow::read_parquet("../data/manifestos_meta.parquet")
agg_long   <- arrow::read_parquet("../data/cross_llm_long.parquet")
# agg_long columns: doc_id, model, dim, score, n_chunks, mean_confidence
TRUE
```

```{webr-r}
#| label: run-regression
# Reactive inputs from OJS:
#   ojs_countries (vector of country names; "(all)" means no filter)
#   ojs_year_min  (single int)
#   ojs_models    (vector of model ids)
#   ojs_within    (within-manifesto aggregation choice — already applied upstream)
#   ojs_between   (between-model aggregation choice)
#   ojs_outcome   (dim id)

# Filter sample
sub_meta <- manifestos
if (!"(all)" %in% ojs_countries && length(ojs_countries) > 0) {
  sub_meta <- sub_meta |> filter(countryname %in% ojs_countries)
}
sub_meta <- sub_meta |> filter(year >= ojs_year_min)

# Apply between-model aggregation across the chosen models
combine_models <- function(x, method) {
  if (length(x) == 0 || all(is.na(x))) return(NA_real_)
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  switch(method,
    mean             = mean(x),
    median           = median(x),
    leave_worst_out  = if (length(x) <= 2) mean(x) else mean(sort(x)[-which.max(abs(x - mean(x)))]),
    mean(x)
  )
}

target_dims <- c(ojs_outcome, "populism_overall")
df <- agg_long |>
  filter(model %in% ojs_models, dim %in% target_dims,
         doc_id %in% sub_meta$doc_id) |>
  group_by(doc_id, dim) |>
  summarise(score = combine_models(score, ojs_between), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = dim, values_from = score) |>
  inner_join(sub_meta |> select(doc_id, countryname, year, partyabbrev), by = "doc_id")

if (nrow(df) < 10) {
  cat("Not enough observations after filtering (n =", nrow(df), "). Loosen the filter.\n")
} else {
  fml <- as.formula(paste0(ojs_outcome, " ~ populism_overall | countryname + year"))
  fit <- feols(fml, data = df, cluster = ~countryname)
  cat("n =", nobs(fit), "  models =", paste(ojs_models, collapse=", "), "\n\n")
  print(summary(fit))
}
```

```{webr-r}
#| label: coef-plot
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
if (exists("fit")) {
  cf <- broom::tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE)
  ggplot(cf, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
    geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), size = 0.8) +
    labs(x = "Coefficient (95 % CI)", y = NULL,
         title = paste0(ojs_outcome, " ~ populism_overall | country + year")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}
```

## Specification curve preview

Below is the distribution of `populism_overall` coefficients across the
~9,072 defensible analytic specifications described in
[methodology](../methodology.qmd). Currently a precomputed static image —
will become live-filterable in v1.1.

![Spec curve placeholder — generated after final corpus run](../assets/spec_curve_placeholder.png)
